question: Is the intended use of the system written down somewhere?
Does the organisation know what data the system was trained on?
Are the boundaries of the system — what it will and will not do — defined?
Does the organisation know what inputs the system receives?
Does the organisation know what outputs the system produces?
Are the known limitations of the system documented?
Is there a record of what the system is not designed to do?
Does the organisation know whether any part of the system was built or supplied by a third party?
Has the system ever been assessed for bias in its outputs?
Can someone in the organisation explain why the system produced a specific output in a specific case?
Does the organisation know how well the system performs — its accuracy and error rates?
Is there a process for identifying when the system is being used outside its intended scope?
When the system is updated, is the change documented?
Is there any evidence that the system's outputs are fair across different groups of people?
Is there documentation describing what the system is, how it works, and what it is for?
If the system produced a wrong output today, would anyone in the organisation know about it?
Has anyone checked whether the system behaves differently for some groups than others?
Is there a named individual who is accountable for this system?
Is that accountability written down — not just understood informally?
Does the accountable person know they are accountable?
Is it clear who has the authority to approve changes to the system?
Does the board know this system exists and what it does?
Is there a defined escalation path if something goes wrong?
If the accountable person left tomorrow, would their responsibilities transfer clearly?
Are roles and responsibilities defined specifically for this system — not just for the organisation generally?
Is it clear who is responsible for the system when a vendor supplies part of it?
Is there a gap between what governance documents say and what actually happens in practice?
Does management receive regular information about how the system is performing?
Is authority to act formally delegated — not just assumed?
If something went wrong today, could the organisation identify within one hour who is responsible?
Has anyone ever actually been asked to answer for something this system did?
Could the organisation point to a document that says who is in charge of this system?
Is the system monitored while it is running?
Are there alerts that fire when the system behaves unexpectedly?
Does a person review the system's outputs — or does it run without human attention?
Can a person override a decision the system has made?
Is there a defined process for handling cases the system was not designed for?
If the system produced an obviously wrong output today, would anyone notice?
Is there a process for escalating concerns about the system's behaviour?
Can the system be stopped quickly if needed?
Has the system been tested to confirm it works as intended?
When the system is changed, is it tested before the change goes live?
Are operational reviews of the system held on a regular basis?
Does the person responsible for oversight have the authority to act — not just to observe?
Is there a record of when the system was last reviewed?
If the system needed to be stopped right now, does anyone know how to do it?
Can the organisation reconstruct what the system did in a specific case?
Are records of individual decisions kept?
Are system logs retained — and does the organisation know for how long?
If a decision from six months ago was challenged today, could the organisation explain it?
Are complaints about the system recorded systematically — not just handled and forgotten?
Would the organisation's records survive a regulatory investigation?
Is there documentation that describes what the system is and how it works?
Are incident records kept when something goes wrong?
Is evidence preserved after an incident — before it can be overwritten or lost?
Are records accurate — or do they describe what should have happened rather than what did?
Does the organisation know how long it is legally required to keep records of this system's decisions?
Is there a record of what the system was doing at the time of a past incident?
Can the organisation produce a complete record of a specific decision — inputs, output, and what happened next?
If a journalist asked what decision the system made about a named individual, could the organisation answer?
Does the organisation know which regulator oversees this system?
Do individuals affected by the system's decisions have legal rights — and does the organisation know what they are?
Can an affected individual request a human review of a decision made about them?
Does the organisation know whether this system is classified as high risk under applicable regulation?
Is the organisation aware of its legal record-keeping obligations for this system?
Does the organisation have reporting obligations if something goes wrong — and does it know what they are?
Could a regulator demand to see records of this system's decisions?
Could a regulator order this system to stop operating?
Is the organisation aware of its data protection obligations in relation to this system?
Does the organisation know whether it could be held liable if the system causes harm?
Has the organisation assessed whether the system's outputs could constitute discrimination?
Does the organisation know what consumer protection obligations apply to this system?
If the regulator arrived tomorrow, could the organisation demonstrate how the system is governed?
Has the organisation checked whether any use of this system is prohibited under applicable law?
Is anyone in the organisation responsible for tracking changes in the law that affect this system?
Does the organisation know whether the EU AI Act applies to any of its systems?
Has any system the organisation deploys been assessed for its risk classification under the EU AI Act?
Does the organisation know what a conformity assessment is and whether it has conducted one?
Is there anyone in the organisation whose job includes tracking EU AI Act compliance obligations?
Does the organisation know which of its systems would be classified as high risk under the EU AI Act?
Is the technical documentation required for high-risk systems under the EU AI Act in place?
Does the organisation know that the EU AI Act applies to organisations outside the EU if their systems affect people inside it?
Has the organisation considered whether any of its systems would be prohibited under the EU AI Act?
Does the organisation have a process for updating conformity assessments when systems change significantly?
Is the organisation aware that fines under the EU AI Act can reach seven percent of global annual turnover?
Does the organisation know whether its vendor-supplied AI systems carry EU AI Act obligations for the deployer?
Has human oversight been designed into any high-risk systems as a requirement rather than added afterwards?
Does the organisation know whether any of its AI systems process personal data?
Has a lawful basis for processing personal data been identified and documented for each AI system that uses it?
Does the organisation know that individuals have a right to an explanation of automated decisions made about them?
Can the organisation actually deliver an individual-level explanation of a specific automated decision if asked?
Does the organisation maintain records of its data processing activities as required by data protection law?
Has the organisation defined retention periods for personal data processed by its AI systems?
Does the organisation know that data protection accountability requires it to demonstrate compliance — not just achieve it?
Has the organisation assessed whether its AI systems engage in profiling of individuals?
Does the organisation know which data protection authority supervises its processing activities?
Could the organisation respond to a data subject access request relating to an automated decision within the legal timeframe?
Does the organisation know that GDPR fines can reach four percent of global annual turnover?
Has the organisation considered data minimisation — whether its AI systems use only the data they actually need?
Has the organisation assessed whether any of its AI systems influence decisions about people in protected categories?
Does the organisation know that discrimination law applies to outcomes — not just intentions?
Has the system ever been tested to check whether its outputs differ systematically across demographic groups?
Does the organisation understand what indirect discrimination is and how AI systems can produce it?
Has the organisation checked whether any input variables used by the system act as proxies for protected characteristics?
Does the organisation know that training on historical data can embed historical discrimination into its systems?
Are records kept that would allow the organisation to demonstrate non-discriminatory outcomes if challenged?
Has the organisation assessed discrimination risk for systems used in hiring, credit, housing or benefits decisions?
Does the organisation know which equality law applies in each jurisdiction where its systems operate?
Is there a process for reviewing system outputs if a discrimination complaint is received?
Does the organisation understand that it cannot defend a discrimination claim simply by showing the system treated everyone the same way?
Does the organisation know whether any of its AI systems are used in consumer-facing decisions?
Are consumers told when they are interacting with an automated system rather than a person?
Does the organisation know whether its recommendation systems are optimised for the consumer's benefit or the organisation's revenue?
Has the organisation assessed whether its AI-driven pricing could be considered manipulative or deceptive under consumer law?
Does the organisation know what dark patterns are and whether its systems use them?
Are consumers given accurate information about what the AI system does and how it works?
Does the organisation know that consumer protection liability attaches to the deploying organisation — not the vendor who built the system?
Are records kept of what the system showed specific consumers — prices, recommendations, options presented?
Has the organisation checked whether its AI systems comply with consumer protection law in every jurisdiction where they operate?
Does the organisation know which consumer protection regulator has jurisdiction over its AI-driven consumer interactions?
Does the organisation know whether any of its AI systems could be classified as a product under applicable law?
Is the organisation aware that the EU's revised Product Liability Directive explicitly covers AI software?
Has the organisation documented the conditions of safe use and known limitations of its AI systems?
Does the organisation know that a product can be legally defective even if it operates within its technical specifications?
Has the organisation assessed the failure modes of its AI systems and documented what happens when they go wrong?
Does the organisation understand its product liability exposure when it integrates third-party AI components into its systems?
Are warnings and instructions about the system's limitations provided to users in a form they can act on?
Is there a process for identifying and reporting safety-relevant AI system failures?
Has the organisation considered post-market surveillance obligations for AI systems embedded in physical products?
Does the organisation know whether its AI systems are subject to sector-specific product safety standards?
Does the organisation use AI systems to set prices or respond to competitor pricing?
Does the organisation understand that algorithmic pricing coordination can breach competition law even without explicit agreement?
Has the organisation assessed whether using the same pricing algorithm as competitors creates competition law risk?
Are records kept of algorithmic pricing decisions and the data inputs that drove them?
Is the board aware of the competition law implications of the organisation's algorithmic pricing systems?
Does the organisation monitor its algorithmic pricing outputs for patterns that could attract regulatory attention?
Does the organisation know which competition authority has jurisdiction over its pricing algorithms?
Has the organisation considered whether AI-driven market behaviour could be interpreted as abuse of a dominant position?
Does the organisation understand that competition law liability attaches to the organisation — not the algorithm that made the pricing decision?
Could the organisation produce documentation of what its pricing algorithms were doing, and why, if a competition authority asked?
Does the organisation know whether any of its AI systems are used in regulated financial services activities?
Can the organisation explain the basis for individual credit decisions, investment recommendations or other regulated financial decisions made by AI?
Are records of AI-driven financial decisions maintained to the standard that financial regulators require?
Does the organisation have a named individual accountable for AI systems used in financial services?
Has the organisation conducted model risk management assessment for AI systems used in credit, trading or risk functions?
Does the organisation know its obligations under DORA or equivalent operational resilience frameworks?
Has the organisation assessed the governance of third-party AI providers used in financial services as required by DORA?
Are AI systems used in financial advice or investment recommendations meeting applicable suitability standards?
Does the organisation know that financial services regulators have published specific expectations for AI governance?
Can algorithmic trading systems be stopped immediately if they begin behaving unexpectedly?
Does the organisation know which financial regulator has jurisdiction over its AI-driven activities in each market?
Does the organisation operate within a sector that is classified as critical infrastructure in any jurisdiction?
Has the organisation assessed the cybersecurity risks specific to its AI systems — including adversarial attack?
Does the organisation have mandatory incident reporting obligations for AI-related failures under NIS2 or equivalent frameworks?
Can the organisation take its AI systems offline quickly without causing cascading failure to dependent services?
Has the organisation assessed the concentration risk created by dependence on a single AI provider for essential services?
Does the organisation know its supply chain security obligations under applicable cybersecurity frameworks?
Are AI system failures that affect essential service operations reported to the relevant authority within required timeframes?
Has the organisation tested its business continuity plans for scenarios involving AI system failure?
Does the organisation monitor AI systems in critical service contexts for signs of adversarial manipulation — not just malfunction?
Is there a named individual responsible for the cybersecurity of AI systems used in essential services?
Does the board know what AI systems the organisation operates and what risks they present?
Has the board received information about AI governance that is sufficient to exercise meaningful oversight?
Does the organisation know that approving AI deployment without adequate risk assessment may breach director duties?
Are board minutes an accurate record of the AI governance questions asked and the answers received?
Is there a named senior individual accountable for AI systems in each area of the business?
Does the board receive regular management reporting on AI system performance and governance?
Has the organisation considered whether its AI governance arrangements meet the expectations of institutional investors and governance codes?
Does the board know that relying on management assurances without scrutiny does not constitute informed business judgement?
Is there a gap between what the governance framework says about AI oversight and what the board actually receives?
Does the organisation operate in a sector with an individual accountability regime — and has AI governance been included within it?
Is the organisation publicly listed or does it make public statements that could affect investor decisions?
Has the organisation assessed whether its AI systems represent a material risk that must be disclosed to investors?
Does the organisation know what AI washing is and whether any of its investor communications could be characterised as such?
Are claims about AI capabilities made in investor communications grounded in documented evidence?
Does the board review AI-related disclosures before they are published?
Would the organisation be able to demonstrate that investor communications about AI were accurate at the time they were made?
Does the organisation know that a material AI system failure may constitute inside information requiring prompt disclosure?
Has the organisation included AI-related risks in its required risk factor disclosures?
Does the organisation know that securities fraud liability can attach to individuals — not just the company?
Are internal records about AI system performance consistent with what the organisation tells investors about those systems?
Does the organisation know whether any of its AI systems are deployed in a regulated sector?
Has the organisation identified which sector-specific regulator has oversight of its AI systems?
Does the organisation understand that AI systems performing regulated functions must meet the standards of that sector — not just general AI regulation?
In healthcare settings, do clinicians understand that professional accountability for clinical decisions remains with them — not the AI system?
Has AI used in medical functions been assessed as a medical device under applicable regulation?
Does the organisation have post-market surveillance in place for AI systems deployed in regulated sectors?
Are failures of AI systems in regulated sectors reported through the sector's mandatory reporting mechanisms?
Does the organisation know that sector regulators may have specific AI governance expectations beyond those in general AI regulation?
Is technical documentation for AI systems in regulated sectors maintained to the standard the sector regulator requires?
Has the organisation considered that AI failures in regulated sectors may simultaneously trigger multiple legal frameworks?
Does senior management understand the specific AI governance obligations that apply in the sectors the organisation operates in?
Does the organisation know which UK regulator has oversight of this system?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under UK GDPR for automated decision-making?
If the system operates in financial services, has the organisation assessed its obligations under the FCA Consumer Duty?
Has the organisation checked whether the system's outputs could constitute discrimination under the Equality Act 2010?
Is there a named senior manager accountable for this system under the SMCR or Companies Act?
Can the organisation explain a specific automated decision to an individual under UK GDPR Article 22 equivalent rights?
Has the organisation conducted a Data Protection Impact Assessment for this system?
Does the board understand the AI governance risks and its obligations under the Companies Act?
Has the organisation assessed CMA competition concerns if the system influences pricing or market behaviour?
Is the organisation monitoring the UK government's AI regulatory developments that may affect this system?
Has the organisation mapped which federal regulators have jurisdiction over this system?
If the system influences credit decisions, has the organisation assessed its ECOA and CFPB adverse action obligations?
Has the organisation assessed FTC unfair and deceptive practices risk for this system?
If the system is used in employment decisions, has the organisation assessed EEOC discrimination obligations?
Has the organisation assessed which state AI laws apply — Colorado, California, New York or others?
If the system processes health data, has the organisation assessed HIPAA obligations?
Has the organisation assessed SEC disclosure obligations if AI capabilities are material to investors?
Can the organisation provide specific adverse action explanations for AI-driven decisions as required by CFPB guidance?
Has the organisation assessed whether the system's outputs could constitute disparate impact discrimination under Title VII?
Is the organisation tracking the rapidly evolving US state AI legislation landscape?
If the system provides algorithmic recommendations to users in China, has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Algorithmic Recommendation Regulation?
Does the system generate synthetic media or deep synthesis content that requires labelling under Chinese regulation?
If the system is a generative AI service available to users in China, has the organisation completed the required security assessment?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under PIPL for personal data processed by this system?
Does the system process data that must be stored within China under data localisation requirements?
Has the organisation assessed Cyberspace Administration of China registration or filing requirements for this system?
Can affected individuals request human review of decisions made about them under PIPL?
Does the system's content comply with Chinese content requirements including applicable value standards?
Is the organisation aware of the developing comprehensive AI Law and its likely implications for this system?
Has the organisation assessed whether the system's cross-border data transfers comply with Chinese data export rules?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under PIPEDA or applicable provincial privacy law for this system?
If the system processes data of Quebec residents, has the organisation assessed its obligations under Law 25?
Can affected Quebec residents request human review of significant automated decisions about them?
Has a designated individual been identified as accountable for privacy compliance for this system?
If the system is operated by a federal institution, has it been assessed under the Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making?
Has the organisation assessed Competition Bureau concerns if the system influences pricing or market conduct?
Is the organisation monitoring the anticipated new federal AI legislation following AIDA's collapse?
Can the organisation provide meaningful explanation of automated decisions to affected individuals as required under Law 25?
Has the organisation assessed OSFI model risk management obligations if the system is used in financial services?
Does the organisation have a Privacy Impact Assessment for high-risk automated processing?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the LGPD for automated decision-making affecting individuals in Brazil?
Has the organisation classified this system under Brazil's AI Law risk framework?
If classified as high-risk under Brazil's AI Law, has the organisation completed the required conformity assessment?
Can individuals affected by automated decisions request human review under LGPD?
Has the ANPD been notified of high-risk automated processing activities as required?
Has the organisation assessed Consumer Defense Code obligations for consumer-facing AI applications?
If the system operates in financial services in Brazil, has the organisation assessed BCB model governance requirements?
Does the organisation have a named Data Protection Officer as required under the LGPD?
Has the organisation assessed whether the system's outputs could constitute discrimination under Brazilian law?
Can the organisation demonstrate LGPD accountability through documented governance records?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the PDPA for personal data processed by this system?
If the system operates in financial services in Singapore, has the organisation assessed MAS FEAT principles compliance?
Has the organisation assessed MAS generative AI guidance if the system uses generative AI in financial services?
Has the organisation considered AI Verify participation to demonstrate governance maturity?
Is there a named individual accountable for this system's governance as expected under MAS guidance?
Has the organisation assessed fairness across demographic groups as required by MAS FEAT principles?
Are customers informed when AI is used in decisions affecting them, as required by MAS transparency expectations?
Does the organisation maintain records of AI model development, validation and performance as expected by MAS?
Has the organisation notified the PDPC of this system's data processing activities where required?
Does the organisation's PDPA Data Protection Officer have oversight of this system?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Act on Protection of Personal Information for this system?
Has the organisation reviewed METI AI Guidelines for Business in relation to this system?
If the system operates in financial services in Japan, has the organisation assessed FSA supervisory guidance?
Has the organisation assessed Japan Fair Trade Commission concerns if the system influences pricing or market behaviour?
Does the system involve cross-border data transfers that require assessment under APPI third-party provision rules?
Are individuals informed about how their personal information is used in automated processing as required by APPI?
Is the organisation monitoring the implementation regulations expected under Japan's AI Promotion Act?
Has the organisation assessed the Act against Misleading Representations if the system makes AI-related marketing claims?
Does the organisation maintain records of personal information processing as required under APPI accountability obligations?
Has the organisation notified the Personal Information Protection Commission of required processing activities?
Has the organisation assessed whether this system falls within the high-impact categories of South Korea's AI Basic Act?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under PIPA for automated decision-making affecting individuals in South Korea?
Is the organisation preparing for the full effect of the AI Basic Act obligations taking effect in 2026?
Can individuals subject to significant automated decisions request human review under PIPA?
Does the system generate AI content that requires disclosure under the AI Basic Act?
If the system operates in financial services in South Korea, has the organisation assessed FSC and FSS guidance?
Has the organisation assessed Korea Fair Trade Commission concerns for algorithmic pricing or market conduct?
Has the organisation notified the Personal Information Protection Commission of high-risk automated processing?
Is there a pre-deployment risk assessment documenting the system's risk classification under the AI Basic Act?
Does the organisation have a documented accountability structure meeting the AI Basic Act's requirements?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act for this system?
Has the organisation identified the Data Fiduciary accountable for this system under the DPDPA?
If the system operates in financial services in India, has the organisation assessed RBI model risk management requirements?
Has the organisation assessed SEBI obligations if the system is used in investment management or algorithmic trading?
Does the organisation have valid consent or another lawful basis for personal data processing under the DPDPA?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Competition Act for algorithmic pricing or market conduct?
Is the organisation monitoring the development of DPDPA implementation regulations?
Has a Data Protection Officer been designated for this system as required for significant fiduciaries under the DPDPA?
Has the organisation assessed fairness implications of this system across India's socioeconomic and demographic diversity?
If the system processes health data, has the organisation assessed obligations under healthcare sector regulation?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Privacy Act for automated decision-making affecting Australians?
Has the organisation assessed Australian Consumer Law obligations for this system?
If the system operates in financial services in Australia, has the organisation assessed ASIC and APRA expectations?
Does the system make any AI-related claims that could constitute misleading conduct under the Australian Consumer Law?
Has the organisation assessed ACCC competition concerns if the system influences pricing or digital market conduct?
If the system provides financial advice, has the organisation assessed ASIC obligations for AI in financial advice?
Has the organisation assessed Corporations Act director duty obligations for board approval of this system?
Is the organisation monitoring the government's proposed mandatory AI guardrails consultation?
Has the organisation assessed the system's outputs for discrimination under Australian equality legislation?
Can the organisation demonstrate its AI governance to the OAIC if an investigation were initiated?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under POPIA for automated decision-making affecting individuals in South Africa?
Has the organisation registered as a responsible party with the Information Regulator as required under POPIA?
Can individuals subject to automated decisions request human review under POPIA Section 71?
Has the organisation assessed constitutional rights implications — equality, dignity, privacy — for this system's outputs?
If the system is used in financial services in South Africa, has the organisation assessed FSCA conduct obligations?
Has the organisation assessed obligations under the Promotion of Administrative Justice Act for public sector automated decisions?
Has a designated Information Officer been appointed as required under POPIA?
Has the organisation assessed Companies Act director duty obligations for the deployment of this system?
Does the organisation maintain records demonstrating POPIA accountability that would satisfy the Information Regulator?
Has the organisation assessed Competition Commission concerns for algorithmic pricing or market conduct?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Nigeria Data Protection Act for this system?
Has the organisation registered with the Nigeria Data Protection Commission as required?
Has the organisation completed a Data Protection Impact Assessment for this automated decision-making system?
Can individuals subject to automated decisions request human review under the NDPA?
Has a Data Protection Officer been designated for this system as required under the NDPA?
If the system operates in financial services in Nigeria, has the organisation assessed CBN model risk requirements?
Does the organisation maintain records of processing activities demonstrable to the NDPC on demand?
Has the organisation assessed FCCPC consumer protection obligations for consumer-facing AI applications?
Are individuals informed about automated decision-making and the logic applied as required under the NDPA?
Is the organisation aware of the NDPC's active enforcement posture and prepared to respond to compliance enquiries?
Has the organisation assessed its obligations under the Data Protection Act 2019 for automated decision-making affecting Kenyans?
Has the organisation registered with the Office of the Data Protection Commissioner as required?
Has a Data Protection Officer been designated for this system as required under Kenya's Data Protection Act?
Can individuals subject to automated decisions request explanation and review under Kenya's Data Protection Act?
If the system operates in financial services in Kenya, has the organisation assessed CBK and CMA obligations?
Has the organisation assessed obligations under Kenya's Consumer Protection Act for consumer-facing AI applications?
Is the organisation monitoring Kenya's Draft AI Code of Practice and anticipated mandatory requirements?
Has the organisation assessed the system's fairness implications under Kenya's constitutional equality provisions?
Does the organisation maintain processing records demonstrable to the ODPC on demand?
Has the organisation assessed Communications Authority obligations if the system operates in telecommunications or digital services?
